text.title: graphql-api-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for graphql-api-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Resolver eagerly loads all related entities in the parent query, regardless of whether the client requested them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Executes DB queries for data the client never uses, degrading performance proportionally to the number of unused relations loaded.

failure-mode.description: List field returns all items with no pagination: async def users(self) -> List[User].
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Returns millions of rows in one request on a large dataset. All list fields that could grow must use cursor-based Connection types with server-enforced size caps.

failure-mode.description: Mutations raise exceptions for business errors (validation, not found, conflict) instead of returning typed error payloads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exceptions become opaque errors[] entries with no type information. Clients cannot distinguish "user not found" from "permission denied" without string-matching error messages.

failure-mode.description: Schema breaking changes (renaming or removing a field) merged without a deprecation cycle and schema diff check in CI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Clients that query the renamed/removed field receive a GraphQL error. Breaking changes require @deprecated(reason: "..."), usage analytics to confirm zero traffic, then removal.
